It is good for those who are blessed with the new birth to consider that they were conceived in sin. This should lead us to have low opinions of ourselves and high opinions of God’s grace. It is the greatest comfort to be made useful for the glory of God. The more holiness people have, and the more good they do, the happier they are. Let us seriously reflect on our guilt. Doing so will help keep our hearts humble and our consciences alert and sensitive. It makes Christ more precious to the soul and gives strength to our efforts and prayers for others.

The gospel of Christ will be preached and published. How can we escape if we ignore it? There is no salvation without righteousness. The soul will, as far as this world is concerned, vanish like smoke, and the body be discarded like a worn-out garment. But those whose happiness is in Christ’s righteousness and salvation will have the comfort of it when time ends. Clouds darken the sun, but do not stop its course. The believer will enjoy his portion while people who insult Christ are in darkness.

The people whom Christ has redeemed with his blood as well as by his power will obtain joyful deliverance from every enemy. If he plans such joy for us eventually, will he not work such deliverance during our lives, as our situations require? In this world of changes, it is a short step from joy to sorrow, but in that world, sorrow will never be seen again. They prayed for the display of God’s power; he answers them with consolations of his grace. If we feared sinning against God, we would not fear the disapproval of people. Happy is the person who always fears God. And Christ's church will enjoy security by the power and care of the Almighty.

God calls on his people to remember things that belong to their everlasting peace. Jerusalem provoked God and was made to taste bitter consequences. Those who should have been her comforters were their own tormentors. They lack patience to keep possession of their own souls, nor any confidence in God’s promise by which to keep possession of its comfort. You are intoxicated, not as before, with the intoxicating cup of Babylon's idolatries, but with the cup of affliction. Know that for a time, the cause of God’s people may seem lost, but God will protect it, by convincing consciences or thwarting the plans of those who oppose it. The oppressors demanded that souls be subjected to them, so that everyone would believe and worship as they wanted. But all they could gain by force was outward, hypocritical conformity, because consciences cannot be forced.
